markdown_content:
## Executive Summary

Structured biweekly technology watch report for Y-OS MCP connectors using 3-layer filtering methodology. Analyzed 4 major sources (mcp.so, smithery.ai, GitHub, glama.ai) to identify top 7 Y-OS-relevant connectors and 3 emerging signals. Key recommendations include activating Context7, Knowledge Graph Memory, and GitHub MCP for immediate integration. Report published to Notion with actionable connector recommendations categorized as Activate/Monitor/Ignore.


## Context & Intent

Execute systematic technology surveillance for Y-OS ecosystem to identify valuable MCP connectors and emerging capabilities that could enhance the 7 active agents (CORE, KMM, FIN, PROJ, CREA, TECH, COMM).


## What Was Done

Implemented 3-layer filtering methodology: Layer 1 (objective market traction scoring), Layer 2 (Y-OS relevance filtering), Layer 3 (horizon scanning for weak signals). Scraped 4 MCP sources, analyzed against existing 27 native connectors and 8 custom APIs, produced structured report.


## Outputs Produced

- [report] 🔌 RADAR-MCP Report April 1st 2026 — Comprehensive Notion page with 7 Y-OS-relevant MCP recommendations, 3 emerging signals, top 3 market leaders, and connector registry updates
- [recommendations] MCP Integration Roadmap — Prioritized action plan with Activate/Monitor/Ignore classifications for identified connectors

## Key Decisions & Validations

- Immediate activation recommended for Context7, Knowledge Graph Memory, and GitHub MCP
- Monitor status assigned to Chrome DevTools, Figma, E2B Code Execution, and Alpha Vantage MCPs
- Identified 3 emerging capability areas: temporal knowledge graphs, meta-cognitive reasoning, physical world parsing

## Lessons Learned

Worked well:

- 3-layer filtering methodology effectively balanced market signals with strategic relevance
- Parallel source scraping enabled comprehensive market coverage
- Integration with existing Notion connector registry provided context
Discoveries:

- Sequential Thinking MCP represents transition toward reflective agent autonomy
- PaddleOCR signals agents beginning to 'read' physical world
- Graphiti introduces temporal reasoning to knowledge graphs

## Open Questions

- How will temporal knowledge graphs impact existing KMM agent architecture?
- Should Y-OS develop meta-cognitive capabilities similar to Sequential Thinking MCP?
- What integration complexity exists for the recommended MCPs?

## Next Steps

- Activate Context7 and Knowledge Graph Memory MCPs
- Finalize GitHub PAT configuration for GitHub MCP integration
- Monitor emerging signals for next biweekly cycle
- Update connector registry with new MCP evaluations
---
